metadata.note: Verification run directly in WSL Ubuntu 24.04 (no Docker container)

host_machine.cpu_model: Intel(R) Core(TM) (24 vCPUs visible in WSL)

verification_env.note: NTFS (/mnt/d/) caused build failures due to case-insensitivity (build file vs build directory conflict)

value: CANN / Ascend NPU SDK (NOT available - REQUIRED for full build)

Third-party C++ deps: boost 1.87.0, grpc 1.60.0, protobuf, abseil-cpp, openssl 3.0.9, zlib, re2, cares, prometheus-cpp 1.3.0, spdlog 1.15.3, nlohmannJson, cpp-httplib, gloo, libboundscheck, googletest, mockcpp, makeself, hseceasy

build_commands.value: bash build.sh 3rd (third-party) && bash build.sh master (main source)

execution_log.command: Read README.md, CMakeLists.txt, build.sh, contributing.md, docs/zh/developer_guide/build_guide.md, docs/zh/developer_guide/test.md
execution_log.output: Extracted build dependencies, commands, and test procedures

execution_log.command: pip install --break-system-packages wheel setuptools pybind11 posix_ipc torch

execution_log.error: grpc: file failed to create directory 'build' because File exists (Bazel BUILD file vs build directory conflict on NTFS)

execution_log.command: Copy repo to /home/<user>/ (ext4), clean build dirs, bash build.sh 3rd
execution_log.note: Ext4 filesystem resolves the build file/directory conflict

execution_log.output: Build interrupted; WSL service crashed (resource exhaustion)

execution_log.command: bash build.sh master (8 parallel jobs, MAX_COMPILE_CORE_NUM=8)
execution_log.error: mindieservice_daemon: undefined reference to HealthChecker::EnqueueErrorMessage(...). Root cause: health_checker.cpp.o is 0 bytes (compilation silently failed due to missing include path or dependency)

execution_log.command: Manual compilation test, symbol inspection (nm), include path analysis
execution_log.output: Confirmed health_checker.cpp.o is 0 bytes. Build system includes correct paths but compilation fails silently.
execution_log.error: Unable to resolve within time window. Likely a build system issue exposed in CPU-only build mode.

execution_log.output: Only ATB models example exists (requires Ascend NPU hardware)
execution_log.error: Cannot execute samples without NPU hardware and CANN

execution_log.output: Tests exist but require DOMAIN_LAYERED_TEST=ON and NPU-dependent modules
execution_log.error: Unit tests not executable due to daemon link failure blocking test build

process_timeline.action: Checking WSL environment specs and available tools
Reading README.md, CMakeLists.txt, build.sh, docs, dependency.json
Installing Python build dependencies (pybind11, torch, etc.)

details.error: grpc BUILD file vs build directory conflict on NTFS

process_timeline.action: Copying repo to WSL ext4 filesystem to resolve NTFS issues
Building third-party deps on ext4 (fresh build)
Building core C++ source (24 parallel jobs)

details.error: WSL service crashed due to resource exhaustion

process_timeline.action: Building core C++ source (8 parallel jobs)

details.reason: All examples require Ascend NPU hardware + CANN
Tests require DOMAIN_LAYERED_TEST=ON which is blocked by daemon link failure

build.summary: 17 of 18 C++ targets built; 19 of 19 third-party packages built
failed_targets: mindieservice_daemon (executable) - linker error: undefined reference to HealthChecker::EnqueueErrorMessage

ut.summary: Unit tests could not be executed
ut.blocked_reason: DOMAIN_LAYERED_TEST=ON would trigger test build, but main build (daemon target) fails first, blocking all downstream targets. Also many tests require NPU hardware.

sample.summary: Sample programs could not be executed
sample.blocked_reason: The only sample (examples/atb_models) requires Ascend NPU hardware, CANN SDK, torch_npu, and ATB runtime. None of these are available in the WSL environment.

documentation_gaps: Python version requirement: docs specify Python 3.10/3.11, but code compiles with Python 3.12 (used 3.12.3 successfully for most targets)

NTFS incompatibility: Build fails on NTFS/WSL cross-filesystem because gRPC source contains a Bazel BUILD file that conflicts with the required 'build' directory. Document should mention ext4 requirement for WSL users.
Missing CANN dependency documentation: README.md does not mention that CANN (Ascend NPU SDK) is required for full build. Only found implicitly in CMakeLists.txt conditional logic.
No standalone unit test instructions: docs/zh/developer_guide/test.md is a placeholder with no actual test execution steps.
Incomplete dependency list in docs: README references external doc site; no inline list of build dependencies. Third-party deps only discoverable via dependency.json.
WSL-specific guidance missing: No documentation for building in WSL environment. NTFS issues not documented.
health_checker.cpp compilation issue: Produces 0-byte object file in CPU-only build; root cause not documented.

problems_encountered.problem: gRPC third-party build failure: 'build' file (Bazel BUILD) conflicts with 'build' directory on NTFS
problems_encountered.solution: Relocate project to ext4 filesystem (/home/<user>/ within WSL). NTFS is case-insensitive and cannot distinguish file 'build' from directory 'build'.
problems_encountered.source: Observed behavior; documented as NTFS limitation

problems_encountered.problem: WSL service crash during parallel build (24 jobs on 15Gi RAM)
problems_encountered.solution: Reduce parallelism to 8 jobs (MAX_COMPILE_CORE_NUM=8). Successfully completed 17/18 targets.

problems_encountered.problem: mindieservice_daemon linker error: undefined reference to HealthChecker::EnqueueErrorMessage
problems_encountered.solution: Unresolved. Root cause: health_checker.cpp.o is 0 bytes. Compilation fails silently, likely due to missing CANN-dependent headers or conditional compilation paths that don't apply in CPU-only mode.
problems_encountered.source: Investigation: nm inspection, manual compilation test, include path analysis

problems_encountered.problem: Cannot run examples or tests: require Ascend NPU hardware
problems_encountered.solution: N/A - hardware dependency. Full verification requires Ascend NPU device with CANN installed.
